column9: They want to know: how long are they gong to be there. how long do they think theyre going to be there. does their insurance work (financial compnents). This company wont accpet them unles their insurance does work. once facility knows clinically and financailly they can cover them, then facility takes them.
column10: Facility will mention after a certain amount of days what the co-pay will be. Families want to know what medications the paitent is on. want to know therapy progress. also want to know how paitent is participanting in activites and meetings.
column11: facility would like to know the families gameplan for after they get better (discharge plan). discharge plan should start the day theyre admitted. Assisted living vs boarding care: Acute hospital is high level of care, SNF for continuation of care, (hospital has 1 nurse for every paitent, then SNF has 1 nurse for every 6-8 paitents) if they still need care after SNF they go to an assisted living facility. sometimes they go to a board and care (house with a few rooms for rent for people who meet health department criteria, doesnt need to be a nurse but needs to be someone on watch).

column6: Challenges: records from hospital from prior admissions. A lot of the contact info from hospital is outdated so they don’t know who is the responsible party. “We’re only as good as the paperwork they come with”. If a resident is alert and oriented they ask them who is the next of kind or partner. If they can’t get it from the resident they try to figure out who their doctor is. If all else fails they reach out to a “doctor of aging”.
